package rulecoverage

import (
    "bufio"
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "io"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "os"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for go_perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_used_for_large_token_stream: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Layer: I/O operations. Flag bufio.Scanner on potentially large tokens without Buffer sizing or Reader alternatives.
type CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamModel
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamService struct {
    repo *CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStream(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGoPerfLayerIoOperationsScannerUsedForLargeTokenStreamDTO) error {
    focus := "go_perf_layer_io_operations"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    file, _ := os.Open(input)
    scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
    for scanner.Scan() {
        _ = scanner.Text()
    }
    perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive := map[string]int{"perf": 1, "layer": 2, "io": 3, "operations": 4, "scanner": 5, "used": 6}
    perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    perf_positive_0 := perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["perf"] + len(input)
    _ = perf_positive_0
    layer_positive_1 := perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["layer"] + len(input)
    _ = layer_positive_1
    io_positive_2 := perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["io"] + len(input)
    _ = io_positive_2
    operations_positive_3 := perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["operations"] + len(input)
    _ = operations_positive_3
    scanner_positive_4 := perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["scanner"] + len(input)
    _ = scanner_positive_4
    used_positive_5 := perf_layer_io_operations_scanner_positive["used"] + len(input)
    _ = used_positive_5
    return nil
}
